FY.executive_summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) reported a strong fiscal year, with revenue increasing 6.4% to $416.2 billion, driving net income up 19.5% to $112.0 billion. The company's profitability has seen a notable uptick, with net margin expanding 12.3% to 26.9% and return on equity (ROE) decreasing 7.7% to 151.9%, indicating a slight decline in efficiency despite higher profitability. Meanwhile, Apple's leverage has decreased, with debt-to-equity decreasing by 28.4% to 3.87x, a significant improvement in its capital structure. The company's liquidity position has also shown a modest improvement, with the current ratio increasing 3.0% to 0.89. Looking at the stock, Apple's shares have gained 7.3% over the past month, closing at $317.31. Overall, Apple's financial health appears robust, with its profitability and leverage metrics pointing to a healthy balance sheet.

This sharp drop in profitability resulted in a notable decline in net margin, which decreased by 9.2%, and a substantial decline in return on equity (ROE), which decreased by 41.8%. The company's debt-to-equity ratio decreased by 24.7%, indicating a reduction in leverage. In contrast, the current ratio increased by 9.9%, suggesting an improvement in liquidity. Apple's stock price has recently shown a 7.3% increase over the past month, but the company's overall financial health remains a concern due to its declining profitability. Despite this, Apple's strong brand and loyal customer base may help it recover in the long term.
